rationale: The cited paper is used in the citing paper's theory-building, not just listed as background. In Table 1 and the hypothesis development section, the authors explicitly draw on Antia, Zheng, and Frazier (2013) to argue that visible replacement capacity creates a credible termination threat. That borrowed logic helps justify why their bench program should deter shirking and improve performance. Although they do not reuse the cited paper's data or model, the citation meaningfully informs the conceptual framing of the intervention.
relation_description: The citing paper applies a core idea from the cited franchise study - that ownership-based replacement capacity signals a credible threat of termination - to a sales force context. It uses this analogy to motivate why hiring and training bench replacements should strengthen deterrence.
